g.data-narration: Welcome to the comprehensive guide on Components of E-Waste Management as per E-Waste Management Rules 2022.

E-waste refers to discarded electrical and electronic devices that have reached end of their useful life, including components and spares. These contain hazardous substances like lead and mercury that pollute our environment.
g.text: that have reached end of useful life.

g.data-narration: E-waste is actually a valuable resource containing precious metals like gold, silver, copper and palladium that can be recovered through proper recycling, achieving sixty to eighty percent material recovery.
g.text: E-waste contains precious metals that can be recovered:

g.data-narration: As per the 2022 Rules, e-waste is categorized into large appliances, ICT equipment, consumer electronics, and lighting equipment for proper management and recycling.
E-waste is generated from four main sources: residential households, commercial offices, industrial manufacturing, and institutional establishments like schools and hospitals.
E-waste collection methods include authorized collection centers, manufacturer take-back programs, door-to-door collection services, and periodic event-based drives organized by municipalities.
g.text: Segregation: Separate hazardous from non-hazardous, recyclable from non-recyclable

g.data-narration: Proper e-waste storage requires temporary storage with covering, hazardous material containment, fire safety systems, and security documentation. Transportation needs licensed carriers with proper packaging.
E-waste processing follows three methods: reuse through refurbishment, recycling via dismantling and separation techniques, and secure disposal through landfilling or incineration.
The E-Waste Management Rules 2022 mandate Extended Producer Responsibility, prohibit open burning, require mandatory registration for handlers, and set annual collection targets.
The Central Pollution Control Board at national level coordinates implementation, while State Pollution Control Boards grant authorization and monitor compliance at state level.
